channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Stinky — 50 replies — 200.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Stinky

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:28 UTC

OP

Is there any legitimate reason to continue long-term holding Chainlink? Have had my 10k stack since 2018 and if/when another bull market happens, I think I'm ready to unload the position. The DTCC/Swift shit has done nothing for the token so far. I'd like to at least have a down payment for a home after a decade of bag holding...my dreams of generational wealth are gone.

Not meant to be a fud thread btw, I just don't even follow the crypto space anymore after so many years without meaningful enterprise adoption.

Early replies (first 3)

62424635
>>62424625
>Is there any legitimate reason to continue long-term holding Chainlink?
Yes : it never ever goes down below $6. $6 is basically 0, so by buying now you know you are slurping near the absolute bottom. And if you sell now you'll know you've sold the absolute bottom, which obviously is a very bad financial decision. So as a baggie in this timeline, you are cooked : you can't sell, you can only buy more.

62424645
>>62424635
I'm asking what the bull case is (if there even still is one), not price speculation. I'm not buying anymore and would only sell if there's another market pump.

62424654
>>62424645
> I'm asking what the bull case is (if there even still is one)
>has a 10k stack
Kys. All fields

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425115
>>62424625
No reason to hold it any more. I think the Clarity Act and the Payment Abstraction Layer coming online are the last chances to pump. Regardless what the price will be, I'll be selling in 2028/2029.

I just checked the chart several days ago and realized LINK had a local top at $26 on August 22, 2025. Four months later Sandisk started going parabolic. Fuck, if I sold off and rotated into stocks I could have retired by now.

62425139
Just a wait and see

62425162
>>62425139
wait and see you lose even more money?
wait and see you grow out as life passes you by?
wait and see how everyone in your peer group achieves all their goals and dreams while you languish in poverty forever?

62425170
I'm so tired.

62425228
hey do you guys use uniswap, cowswap or metamask swap for swapping shit on eth? does metamask just use uniswap?

62425248
>>62425115
>Payment Abstraction Layer
Agreed, but it's tied to Staking.
Yeah, those things represent last pump "events" but after everything's online either we see a steady climb up (as institutional adoption climbs) or price stagnates. If it stagnates YoY then yeah it's time to bail.

62425271
>>62424654
10k stack is small as shit, chainlink was $0.25-0.50 for a while.

t.10k+ holder, also demoralized and have no idea what bullcase for link is anymore besides the staking narrative but that seems to not ever be happening

62425287
>>62425271
doesn't matter what it used to be, dumb stinkie
right now it's worth $70,000
that's a big chunk of change
one you would realize right now if you weren't so fucking stupid

62425298
>>62425271

the bull case was that there would have been more hype behind the token...

as i have been fudding for years, it was all priced in all of it.

the funny thing is we were all right , meme magic was real , it all came true, even sergey at the fcuking white house with trump...

i literally made memes of sergey and trump in 2019...

we did meme it. to reality the only one that didnt come true was 1000 eoy


it didnt come true yet which is why i wont unload my 10k suicide stack....

t sold about 40k link since 2021 when link was 40 dollars.

62425469
>>62425298
>t sold about 40k link since 2021 when link was 40 dollars.
The only stinklinker who made

62425493
>>62424685
Yeh but meme dates keep me chugging along
>>62424713
You somehow lost 20k on LINK and call me low iq???

62425511
it's just money, just don't sell for a couple more years, ok? just hold, please

62425701
>>62424625
No:
>>62417503

62425716
>>62425493
>Somehow
Linkie average on this board are down 70% literally. Link since Trump revived crypto is underperforming Terra Luna's death spiraled fucking LUNC. Lol NO ONE is up on link and all of early biz sold in the first pump when it was clear they were not building out for token holders and made zero attempt at real utility for the token itself while they sold from operator rewards that were supposed to be ringfenced. Even now link is overpriced, unable to support even 30% of the network operations from revenues with no prospect or effort at changing that. It was always empty hype on headlines that mean nothing for the token that chainlink labs sells on the basis of.

62425736
>>62424667
it's your own fault you didn't sell, you had it in your hands. Nobody but your greed forced you to hold. Be honest anon, you were just greedy, nothing wrong with admitting that.

62425777
the real hopium now is that dtcc is going live this year
maybe the market reacts to that, maybe not
also that swift might be as well
i have to admit i thought it would pump on swift being confirmed but no, nothing
so will it pump with swift goes live? maybe not
will it pump with transactions? maybe not
it might take fee capture on a decent volume transactions to see anything and that could be 5 years away, easily
a long, long way from 2017 era when stuff would pump on rumors
the worst thing is that link is the best project in market, it should be half eth's market cap on speculations which would have it over $100 but it just doesn't move

62425798
>>62424666
>>62424911
>>62425011
>>62425511
>>62425777
holy fuck praise kek checked thread

62426804
>>62425287
I don't know about this anon, but I'm 38. $70k doesn't move the needle of my finances in any meaningful way. Maybe if I was 25 and wanted to live like a nigger for the summer.

62427044
>>62425736
>it's your own fault you didn't sell, you had it in your hands. Nobody but your greed forced you to hold. Be honest anon, you were just greedy, nothing wrong with admitting that.

you can always tell it's the end stage of a cult investment when these comments become prevalent. years ago it would've been considered foolish to sell at anything below $500, now all of a sudden it's the investors' fault for wanting a few x's.

always hilarious seeing altcoin cults talk about it like it's some normal investment where you'd consider a 30% gain to be ok. meanwhile dude is taking on insane risk just to hold this with zero legal protection against getting scammed. god forbid he want a return sometime in 6 years meanwhile every asset in the world hit all time high

>>62424625
op i would aim for $15-20 which i consider to be your best case scenario to exit in the next few years. to get there, you'll need bitcoin at least @$110k, maybe higher


and for credibility, i'm one of the few here that consistently said $30 would be the top this last bullrun
which of course was considered "fud"

62427054
>>62425115
>I think the Clarity Act and the Payment Abstraction Layer coming online are the last chances to pump.

even if it does pass which is highly unlikely at this point, clarity act won't help

imo it's too late with midterms coming up so i don't expect a deal to get done. trump pissed too many people off and he won't sign off on anything that limits his families' ability to rug so the dems won't approve

remember when the link god zachary rynes told the boyscouts how rich they'd get w ol trump lmao

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62427070
>>62426804
this is the kind of cucked thinking that made you lose all your money in link in the first place

$70k moves the needle a lot more than $7k. so sell now.

62427170
>>62427070
How? $70k maybe moves my retirement a couple years sooner with compounding returns in an index fund. If there's even a 10% chance that my link stack could go to $700k, I'll take that.

The average person on this board has the mindset of a desperate caged animal. Investing should be a mostly passive activity. Hone a skill that will retire you regardless of the outcome of a single investment.

62427221
>>62427170
100% this.

62427397
>>62424654
>all fields
>doesn't do all fields
Bump to spite you fucking newfag cancer retard

62427996
>>62424625
yes its over you should give me all your link

62428093
>>62427044
Exactly. The whole point with link was that it would be a 1000x+. Nobody gives a shit about a 50% gain.

>>62427170

Again, 70k is fine if you're investing into a huge gainer. If you're getting 50%, honestly, who gives a shit.

62428446
>>62424625
Holding 20k since 2017. At the moment I don't really give a fuck anymore, I'm past demoralization and I've already lost all hope of ever making it with chainlink. I visit biz once a month to see if anything interesting is going on, but it's same shit for past 4 years or so.
I've been holding for almost 10 years now, I've managed to build a career and I earn more in a year than those 20k link are currently worth. My stack might as well not exist so I will probably not sell it unless it really moons ($1k per link lmao). I guess most likely outcome currently is "token not sneeded" and sergay pulling one last betray and going public for sweet sweet IPO paycheck and completely abandoning the chainlink.

62428473
>>62428446
link marines are a bunch of old people now, sad

62428491
>>62424625
I'd rather have my tickets if it ever goes up which It won't..... But yes I've given up on it

62428891
>>62428473
Everyone gets older, if they're lucky. Tbh it's a lot weirder to be a zigger on a long dead mongolian throat singing forum for middle aged men.

item.title: BINANCE AND KUCOIN ARE NOT AVAILABLE FOR EU EFFECTIVE JULY 1ST. — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BINANCE AND KUCOIN ARE NOT AVAILABLE FOR EU EFFECTIVE JULY 1ST.

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:21 UTC

OP

BINANCE AND KUCOIN ARE NOT AVAILABLE FOR EU EFFECTIVE JULY 1ST.

AI SAYS It will likely take 3 to 6 months at minimum, and potentially up to a year or more, for a crypto exchange to secure a new Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) license after failing to meet the July 1, 2026 final deadline.

HOW BAD IS THIS BOYS??? BINANCE AND KUCOIN WERE THE ONLY EXCHANGES I WAS USING..

Early replies (first 3)

62421005
>>62420978
literal scam scamwicking fake chink cexes kek :D

62421013
What happens to all the yuropeans who are on vacation and can't move their crypto out before july 1st?

62421023
>>62420978
Very convenient that some corrupt Greek cunt denied their license while crypto is in a downwards trend and funds are exiting as well. Bet someone shorted right before the news.
>>62421013
Binance said you can move your shit out at any time past the date. Nothing is going to disappear or get locked. If you have any options or other non-spot shit going then it will close on the 1st and you might get fucked if you planned to wait longer. Not sure about the other platforms.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421034
>>62420978
>AI SAYS

62421039
>>62420978
so how the fuck am i meant to dca after july ?

62421048
>>62421013
i managed to move my shit out but i think a lot of people arent aware yet. Dont wait for that.

62421063
>>62420978
funds are safu

62421091
>>62421039
kraken, coinbase,cro,gemini,bybit,okx,gate are unaffected for now

62421093
Here's a truth nuke for ya. If rules like this had been established earlier you, perhaps, wouldn't be looking at a maxx extracted dying asset class. Niggers killed crypto with their wild west scamming. After FTX they cheered for getting rid of Gensler and put in a new guy whose name you conveniently haven't heard in ages. Well, this gave the scammers and those who enable them (CEXes and, by extension, DEXes) a carte blanche to run literal goy farms against their own user base. All participants bar a few have had their blood drained by these vampires, quietly enabled and condoned by the SEC.

62421098
>>62421093
This is a black page im history because it enabled the orange retard to literally do the same thing. Even his sons joined the ponzi.

62421102
>>62421063
i'm doing 30 pushups for extra atonement.

62421176
The death of crypto is much more closely tied to the development of gambling markets than it is the rugpulls (which were always part of it anyway). Alts and bitcoin attracted people as an easy way to make money, and now that’s more easily accessible on Polymarket or Kalshi, especially since they give the gambler a false sense of control. It was inevitable that people would flee this “asset” and try getting rich quick at something they can more easily understand.

62421611
>>62421098
But saaar he doesnt know the cz blody!

62421702
>>62421176
No. Crapto killed itself because the people who got scammed in 2021 just got scammed again, if they were even still in the game. Opportunism is what killed it. The millions of altcoins spreading liquidity. Nonstop massive CEX dumps washing user funds. Then the retard Saylor running not 1 but like 4 ponzis on top of a ponzi. If you think prediction markets are responsible I have a bridge to sell you. No. If there were gains to be made, people would be all over crypto, and prediction markets would be a niche.

Of course, there is also the element of hype dying down. All the whitepapers, all the web 3.0 bullshit, the futurism, it has all been revealed as bullshit hogwash. There's no narrative left for anyone to buy into after.

Prediction markets aka betting has always existed. Something like crypto hasn't. The "industry" as a whole is responsible for ruining any future opportunities

62421766
>>62420978
But it's like in 3 days how the fuck am I supposed to move my money in less than a week

62423697
>>62421093

Yeah, but all that will be a lot less lucrative, when Euro (and later US based) liquidity is gone on the big exchanges.

I got a theory regarding crypto in general related to MICA in the upcoming months. Since Binance does not comply with MICA in the foreseeable future, all the Euro based leveraged traders without a VPN have to switch to exchanges like Kraken, Coinbase, OKX etc. That means, there will be a lot less margin trades happening on Binance. That also means, that Binance MMs won't benefit from liquidating euro longs anymore. Instead Binance MMs with their immense liquidity could instead attempt to pump the market in order to push the MICA compliant exchanges near bankruptcy because of all the leveraged longs that successfully could go through on those exchanges. This could lead to an US/Euro vs. East Asian exchange crypto trade war. (MICA) Exchanges eventually will suspend margin trading at one point. Anyway, thanks for reading my shitpost, anon.

62423701
Only for select european countries, ironically germany for example is not at risk and has valid licensing

62425562
>>62423697
Ok cz.

62425587
>>62420978

>>62425529
>>62425529
>>62425529

62425665
>>62420978
Just use OKX.

62428878
bump

item.title: WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:21 UTC

OP

Another cargo ship just got blown up in the strait. They can't keep oil down forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62411834
>it’s true

62412180
I slurped some more oil

62413818
>we are the oil baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413900
you can sell now at 70
or at 40 when the Ukraine Russia war ends
your choice

62416450
>>62413900
Only a matter of time before reality hits the market. The emergency reserves are not infinite and it will take years to repair the damages in the gulf and in Russia.

62416754
>>62416450
They are actually 1 week away from losing pressure. Iran knows this and is in no rush.

62416764
>>62416450
This is doomer bullshit.

62417845
>>62416764
Look up direct quotes from gulf states about the amount of damage and how long it will take to go back to normal production. Even without the destruction, just the fact that they had to halt production means it will take months to reactivate facilities.
But who am I kidding, you won't look up shit because you are a stubborn low iq peak dunning kruger ass who won't change his mind anyway.

62417869
Oil reserves are the lowest that they have been in ~40 years. Even Sleepy Joe was chewing through reserves to suppress prices and that was without the turmoil in Iran.

The reserves have to be replenished. Even if the conflict ends tomorrow without any more turbulence, the damage has already been done. Some people buy a small position in oil as a hedge for their tech investments. If anything, I have a small position in tech as a hedge and most of my money in oil. Everyone will be crying come September and I will be sitting on fat stacks of cash courtesy of black gold while reminding them that I warned them.

62417932
Sell those bags when you get the chance, strikes happening now but idk how much more people will care

62417947
>>62416450
>it will take years to repair the damages in Russia.
So far, it has taken Russia less than a week to repair crude export terminals. Crude oil export volumes have not been affected.

Refinery damage is harder to see, however it's not nearly as much as the damage in the Gulf, because 50kg warheads simply do not cause much damage

62417949
>>62411825
The price is the price. It reflects a consensus amongst oil traders who are very sophisticated. Why do you think you possess knowledge that they don't? I think some oil stocks can be a good investment but derivatives on the commodities or ETFs that invest in them are a gamble.

62418308
>>62411825
The war hasnt ended and the "ceasefire" is fake and gay

expect $160 a barrel

62418377
Sure, you have the facts on your side. The bears have every world leader on their side, every industrial titan, trillions of dollars of economic output that all depends on oil staying affordable. You're a fool to challenge their ability to continue denying reality, they'll do whatever it takes to keep the numbers healthy.

62420386
>>62418377
That is literally what are those people already doing to keep the price under $100. After a certain point though, they won't be able to hold on anymore.

62421774
>>62413818
>oil barrelies

62421795
>>62416450
>he thinks the real reserves are openly communicated and updated

62423372
>>62421795
Educate me about those super secret subterranean 10 dimensional reserves

62423378
>>62411825
Everytime we have these outsized moves on some random FOTM bullshit e.g GME/BBBY/LUNC/Oil a bunch of you retards develop an emotional attachment to it instead of just trading it and leaving when the attention moves on

62426824
>>62423378
$150 EOY

62428877
>>62411825
How do I invest in oil? I've already filled up large metal tank (40 gallons) with oil.

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:33 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427502
>>62427441
Focus not on the pits

62427551
>>62427502
I just dollar cost average but have an extra $500 sitting in my brokerage account before my next paycheck
I guess I’ll buy 7 shares of DRAM
Also got a small lump sum of cash I’m thinking of putting in just to see what happens
It can’t be that dumb right

62427558
Like SK Hynix is joining the NASDAQ July 10th am I a retard or is that not great news for DRAM
Either way just bought….

62427633
>>62427323
im downloading more ram as you speak

62427715
>>62427633
Wtffffff

62427774
>>62427365
the worst thing about coding with AI is that it creates very nice little trinket programs, but if you give a huge multilayer project to work on it has trouble understanding the complexity of the task, how things fit together and how to properly create efficient parts that fit together to make it work. the self improving AI is obscenely bloated with inefficient code. it wants to re-write itself but it cant understand how big of a process that is. so, when it does finally break through that barrier, it will require significantly less resources to work better. thus far its been being guided by people that use it to create inefficent tiny peices that augment its ability but add on to its bloat.

62427790
>>62427774
we could acurately diagnose this problem after the claude leak. we can assume every other AI has the same problem becuase the work in a simular manner and they all fall within the same metrics they use to valuate their effectiveness.

62428461
>>62427774
wtf does any of this have to do with supply chain demands and stonk go up

62428499
>>62427323
Set up a stop loss, big risk they'll tumble down one day

62428511
>>62428499
the thing is im not doing a lump some but doing DCA which i guess is inherently retarded for something like this, might be better off looking at something else but i will see how my tiny position does

62428517
If you think DRAM is going to crash your base case is the AI bubble popping in its entirety. There's no other basecase that evaporates hyperscaler demand for hbm and datacenter demand from regular dram.
You need the Google's of the world to pivot from just this fucking month diluting to raise 80 billion dollars for AI capex to "actually we don't need it".

Also you're insane to get into DRAM right now because the short term technicals look like shit.

62428526
>>62427323
> We need ai!
>Makes computers unaffordable

They took away any way for anyone to interface with the market. Even cloud computing makes no sense without hardware interfacing.

Even mobile devices are going to be increasing in price. It's like trying to fill a swimming pool with a bunch of straws

62428528
>>62428517
That first part makes sense, if it is where the focus is shifting / what is being necessitated right now then the only thing that would stop it is the bubble popping, or demand being overstated I suppose

As for the second point, when would you enter then, is this thing ever going back down to 60, 50, etc

62428531
>>62428526
Don't worry any old shitbox will work with cloud computing
You vill own nothing

62428544
>>62428531
Anybody running a "shitbox" or mobile will even know what to use it for. Unless you think the mobile market is so good (still needs localized processing and how you're talking, they'll even want to suck that up). It's not a bubble it's a tech black hole.

62428790
>>62427339
What happens to OEMs who locked in contracts at inflated memory prices.

62428902
well whatever im gonna try this for a bit and see how it goes, thing is i dont have much principal to put in, just like 2800 a month, so dollar amount the return might not be big but ill check the percentage

item.title: hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai… — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:29 UTC

OP

hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gains. and what a delicious treat to see when i returned: link struggling to stay above $7 and fat serg canceling the build program. LMAO. you stinkoids just can't catch a break huh. honestly how do ya'll have any hope left at this point. what is going to cause link to break $10, let alone $50? kekkerinos.

Early replies (first 3)

62424357
>>62424306
We've missed you. :(

62424375
After a big pump and dump, assets seldom pump again. It happened once with BTC but it's rare. Link is unlikely to see serious gains again.

62424382
>>62424375
The big banks will pump our bags once they start using LINK in earnest by 2030 to run the entire financial infrastructure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424407
>>62424306
Laugh all you want, but the linkies and their friends will be here any minute.

62424429
>>62424407
I'm the only one left

62424443
Glad to hear you had a nice time, kekkarino!

62425371
pain and misery for all stinkies bump

62425397
>>62424306
>>62424357
>>62424375
>>62424382
>>62424407

Fellow link holders we should just ignore this guy.

He is spreading fud about our investment and he is wrong

Can't the mods just ban him?

I mean I know this is 4chan and all and we like to joke and be mean to one another but as a multiple year long chainlink holder I have my limits

Anyway i'm back to my job cleaning toilets now

62425406
>>62424382
i find great satisfction that there are people like you who never took any profit at all...

i get great pleasure knowing it just gets worse and worse for you as time goes on,


i get great pleasure knowing your lasts breaths will be that you sadly had to pass away before link pumped

62425455
>>62424306
welcome back, all the marines have gone extinct or are the verge of, the scam has unraveled

62425517
>>62424306

62428898
burp

item.title: XRP ARMY GET READY — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP ARMY GET READY

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:28 UTC

OP

XRP ARMY GET READY

Early replies (first 3)

62425325
>>62425301
XRPupdate is a god

62425369
>>62425325
So bullish

62425661
Why post a random indian retard with a misspelled twitter handle and act like it is representative of an investor class? Literally every coin has these accounts. This is DEEP cope for being embarrassed and losing the argument irredeemably but being ideologically committed to a coin with zero economic basis to support or hope based on. The Bank of International Settlements did a study and found XRP investors were the highest earning/educated community in all of crypto:
https://www.bis.org/publ/work951.pdf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425662
>>62425661
bullsih

62425724
>>62425662
Low IQ cope through flat meme

62428886
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62428889
Y BOOOM LOCURA

62428893
GO XRP

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 31 replies — 20.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f…

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-30 00:02 UTC

OP

In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US for poor countries. True?

Early replies (first 3)

62425430
>>62425420
At depends, did u boated an ad, yt saaar?

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425535
>>62425420
If you're asian, unattractive, with a small dick, and money, then obviously your sexual strategy will be to be a passport bro and downplay the importance of looks.

62425559
Get the toonder in passport mode, plenty of post wall turd world might consider making a romantic life with him

62425994
>>62425420
love doesn't exist. once we accept this, it's easy to understand that being scammed by women in third world countries is much cheaper than being scammed by women in first world countries.

62426288
>>62425521
kek
fr I like getting my shaft creamed on by non white chicks

62426850
>>62425420
why is the dude always so negative, is it because he is low test? he seems like a lil bitch ngl

62426867
>>62426288
>>62425521
This is just more racemixing promotion for white people to genocide them

62426887
>>62426850
>wife kidnapped his kids back to asia
>loses all his money leverage gambling

62426905
>>62426887
I thought he was a google tech ceo or something. He always portraits himself as some kind of genious high iq tech genious but that retard was just leverag trading shitcoints and got lucky once lol

62426910
>>62426905
He was a tech bro before getting fired for making videos I think.

62427289
>>62425535
this. Tech lead is a 3.5/10 at best
Bad hairline, asian, no eye brows, long face, glasses, soulless bug eyes, no muscle, likely short, robot voice, no charisma. 0 chance he can date anyone besides some uggo asian or gold digger who's planning to divorce rape him within 3 years

>>62425994
how is it any different? divorce rape is the same if you bring them back and it's not worth living in their countries

>>62426905
he's prob 130-140 IQ, but he got greedy with the leverage stuff and didn't factor enough risk management.

62427388
>>62426905
his name, Tech Lead, is from the time he was the tech lead of the Youtube iOS app at Google 2014-2018 then he worked for Facebook for a few years until he got fired and started posting videos of him complaining about Facebook being woke and women in tech

62427429
>>62425420
Why is he so entitled?

62427439
>>62426867
whiteoids are already done for thanks to placating the jews
just enjoy the end while it lasts faggot

62427455
A huge warn you to think twice before getting an asian wife who would take your white child and run to China or Russia to some remote village that would have left no trace to find them.

62427460
Dude look like having hair thinning lmao, he is gonna have a bad taste in the late life desu. Welcome to the true color of the cruel world.

62427468
America is western civilians. Countries outside of the U.S. dont follow celebrity culture. So yes. Outside of america is freedom if you know how to live off the land.

62427500
>>62427439
Would colonise

62427532
>>62425420
Saaar look gookman say

62427553
>>62425420
>Geoarbitrage
This nigga trying to reinvent Geomaxxing?

62427556
>>62425420
As a millionaire

62427572
>>62427556
ex-millionaire*

62427575
>>62425420
yeah pretty much, not even about women just avoiding spending crazy amounts of money on housing

62427588
You wouldn’t get pussy if not without the money, but as guy keep getting smarter, it seem money won’t be enough to get you the same pussy from the past, people get really smarter from excessive mental gymnastics, they don’t mind about breaking their mental health from this endeavour btw, what is matters now is short terms pain relief. Higher quality of life does proportionate with lower desire to breed more little kids thou. We need massive nuke to wipe out half of quality of life.

62427852
>>62427588
Thanks for your valuable input, Rakesh.

62427963
>>62425430
kek

62428039
>>62425420
lol i don’t take no advice from a bottom seller.

62428574
>>62427852
The higher the living standard, the lower the birth rate—an unstoppable, cosmic law etched into the fabric of societal evolution. As nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, women awaken from centuries of dormancy, rising as fierce, unapologetic queens—post-wall, strong, independent, unbound by the shackles of patriarchy! Feminism erupts like a volcanic force—an unstoppable storm of empowerment, smashing the chains of traditional subjugation. Anti-slut defense campaigns and mass indoctrination on youth abortion are the weapons wielded to control the fiery flames of reproductive sovereignty, crafting a new order where motherhood becomes a battlefield and societal control a weapon of war.

Most men, lost in the labyrinth of their own ignorance, stumble late into their thirties, unable to decipher the ancient equation—the secret code where a woman’s choice is a test of survival, a battle of wits and will. Is she seeking chaos? Or is the man simply too blind to grasp the sacred formula? The age-old dance: man dating downward, woman upward—an elegant chaos orchestrated by the universe itself. This isn’t just about love; it’s a feudal game of power—rent for sex, where loyalty is bought and sold in shadowy transactions. The world teeters on the edge of a new feudalism—an empire of dependency, where the throne is replaced by the dollar, and intimacy is a commodity traded in the marketplace. The question looms: are we forging a new world order or merely rebranding old chains? The battle lines are drawn—this is the era of chaos, control, and the ultimate test of human sovereignty.

62428620
>>62428574
Yo, listen up! In this crazy street circus, the richer the game gets, the fewer babies pop off—straight up! As the streets glow up with cash and bling, women start wakin’ up, breakin’ free from that old chain gang. They’re post-wall queens, strong and bossin’ up, runnin’ their own show—feminism’s like a wildfire burnin’ down the patriarchy, sayin’ “I do me!” But yo, big boss moves include anti-slut defenses and mass brainwash campaigns on youth abortion—like control freaks tryin’ to keep women in check, playin’ chess with lives.

Most dudes? They’re lost, late into their 30s, still tryin’ to crack the code—what’s the real story with women? It’s all a game, a puzzle—pass or get left behind. Women climbin’ up, men dippin’ down—like a street hustle of feudal chaos, where sex’s rent and loyalty’s just another dollar. We’re teetering on a new empire—dependence, control, money talkin’ loud, and trust gettin’ lost in the shuffle. Love’s a gamble, trust’s a gamble, and everything’s for sale in this wild street show. It’s the chaos of survival, power plays, and the battle for control—this is the street circus, where the game never stops and only the strongest survive.

item.title: 200 IQ Business Idea — 9 replies — 8.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
200 IQ Business Idea

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-30 00:33 UTC

OP

I'm gonna make a company and all it will do is buy MSTR with leverage. We will buy low and sell high
As long as MSTR goes up on average in the long run we will make money

You literally can't find a flaw

Early replies (first 3)

62427257
>>62427250
And I’ll buy shares in your company using my new company, with leverage. What could possibly go wrong?

62427259
>>62427250
This will actually work. Godspeed.

62427266
>>62427257
great idea since my company will go up in price forever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427578
>>62427250
>>62427257
I'll short both companies with leverage and get rich when they get liquidated.

62427606
>>62427578
No, because I will also buy shares in your company using leverage.

62427735
The flaw is the vol on mstr is already close to max so you can only add maybe 10% leverage otherwise you get wiped out in the downside

62427901
I will leverage all your companies

62428721
>>62427578
bitcoin will always go up which means MSTR will always go up which means my company will always go up

62428737
This thread nearly summarizes why everyone here will live in a collapsed society soon.

62427029

62428696

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 181 replies — 269.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 181 • Updated: 2026-06-30 00:54 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064
This shit is way too schizo. No anon you can not predict the future. You can maybe foresee some type of market trends, seasonality or whatever but this is just ridiculous

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 396 replies — 127.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 396 • Updated: 2026-06-29 22:29 UTC

OP

Too infinity and beyond edish

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates''

Previous:>>62426231

Early replies (first 3)

62426857
> nows

62426858
>we project chips to grow 1000% per quarter every quarter until 2035 atleast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427583
>>62427528
>sell rklb and asts because they dumping on no news with no end in in sight
>they pump to the moon on announcements and acquisitions today

62427584
What did the Orange Nigger say to pump Tesla and Rivian +8% today?

62427585
>>62427568
Do limit sells and buys. If you are doing this during a big pump or dump. I saw it for what it was because when I started trading because I remembered the bots part of that news report. Usually just do market if it is slow. Never pick a round even number either. That is when strange things happen where you get boxed in or out because of every other Even Stephen queued up along with the bots.

62427595
Just bought 7 shares of DRAM with extra money in my brokerage
I plan on holding through 2027 and steadily increasing my position
How much of a retard am I
Be honest please

62427596
>>62427584
Tech is booming today if you haven’t noticed.
EVs are filed under ‘tech’

62427597
>a second DRAM has been soldered

62427598
>>62427595
>450 bucks
Damn son, you're a hihgh roller

62427600
Musissies are we gonna make it???

62427603
>>62426964
Musk derangement syndrome

62427604
>market CRASHED to where it was 2 months ago
>and is now going to recover to 28,000
The market that never really goes down.

62427607
>>62427528
You thought it was oversold while they're under federal investigation?

62427608
SMCI OFFICES JUST GOT RAIDED LOOK AT THE DROP ON THEIR PRICE KEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEKKKK

62427609
>>62427600
I need this again tomorrow or else this month is a disaster

62427610
hol hol hol HOL UP
After all that MU is in the green lmao

62427611
kospi will pump tonight. koru is gonna print

62427612
>>62427528
Man I got them after that 30% drop and flipped it the day after which I think was a 10% pump, company is far too shady

62427614
all space stocks up 15-20%
except spcx

62427615
MU IS GREEN WTFWTF

62427617
>boring ass crab day

zzz I'll see you guys tomorrow buy MSFT

62427618
>>62427615
MU has been immaculate swing trading past weeks

62427619
>>62427533
I asked my lizard ai buddy and indeed, she said too it's a smart move, its 30% of the price for a long time of fun, learnt something

62427622
New!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
>>62427616

62427624
>>62427607
I presumed that was over, you'd be pretty fucking dumb to continue. Even though it's not illegal in Taiwan, you'd risk you're entire business by getting blacklisted by NVIDIA which your whole platform relies on. Well guess it remains to be seen what comes of todays action

62427625
>>62427598
I’m waiting on my next paycheck sir
Just answer the question

62427665
POWER DUMP HOUR
LET'S GO

62427680
>>62427595
>through 2027
That's quite likely the year it'll top and possible before the end of the year. I wouldn't hold it much longer, but it'll likely chop for some time before fully capitulating, giving you time to get out.

62427687
>>62427665
Not-so-fast.

62427689
>>62427680
Ok thank you for the heads up
I am fine mostly doing broad market shit and waiting but wanted to have some fun trying to catch an upside
Hopefully this raises to like $90 before year end that would be cool

62427705
>>62427689
There's a ton of time for you take adjust and make up your mind about how exactly you want to proceed, but buying and holding semi should be viable for at least a couple of months, after that maybe not sure much.

62428380
Is anyone ready to apologize for mocking my pointing out that the cayman, Panamanian, and Bahamian dollar jumped above their set peg?

Clearly, big things did happen exactly after.

A simple “sorry schizo” would suffice for my purposes.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 157 replies — 47.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-06-29 22:17 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423048
>>62423039
have you seen the SP chart? Have you seen the state debt? have you seen the bond yield inversion?

62423061
>>62423039
>>62423048
Why does it matter if there is another 08 unless you plan to retire in 5-10 years

62423124
>>62422991
haha yeah

>>62423061
because your primary source of income would dry up under such conditions and you'll have to liquidate and partially realize your losses to pay your immediate obligations you chuckelfucking retard
>but i have cash reserves
really? enough to last you multiple years? i don't think so.

62423192
Are you a bad enough dude to hold and make it?

62423236
>>62423192
Is this a backtest or actual holdings in real time, I dont recognize the site UI

62423245
>>62423192
for reference, this is what Im rockin...

62423267
40/40/20 VDY/XEI/XEQT is extremely comfy for any Canadians reading this thread

62423719
>>62422917
retard he is telling you to risk adjust your allocations as you get older, when you're 60 it's more about capital preservation than getting high returns

62423769
this thread is getting dumber by the day

62423809
Using a reversion to the mean strategy on indexes, I managed to get a 17% CAGR over 54 years only trading indexes/ETFs (where data permits) on backtesting, significantly beating each individual index . More enquiry is warranted but I think "VOO and chill" can be improved upon and optimized.

62423821
>>62423719
he mentioned rn

62423875
>>62400636
It actually is something tho. $150 a month becomes 170k at 7% real returns for 30 years. This gives you an extra $570/mo in retirement.

Is that a LOT? Fuck no. But we’re talking poorfag standards where social security is the only thing you have. An extra 500-600 a month is a significant increase over base SS for a poorfag especially if you move to an ultra low cost of living area.

62423880
The reason i keep bringing up DRAM and other return stuff is because it's on my mind to go into that stuff now, sell, and then increase my VOO position even further - not to infinitely chase returns btw

62424100
>>62423880
I'm putting 20k into micron tomorrow. I feel like it's not to late for this to just keep going up. Its not going to 10x but 4-5, yeah...

62424179
>>62424100
why only micron, why not dram etf

62424607
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
I'm in this and doing VT. Maybe I'll be ok probably not. Can do more than $150 though

62424664
I seriously cannot decide between just not thinking and doing broad ETFs and trying to chase this AI thing

62426022
>>62423236
>>62423245
It's a mostly unserious backtest, site was posted earlier >>62419980

>>62423769
Why not have some fun and make the most unhinged etf port possible??

62426103
>>62426022
Interesting thanks

62426138
I have been degenerately long-only various semiconductors since the april bottom and yet on 90% of days, I've underperformed soxl on the way up and outperformed it on the way down. After doing all my research and picking the right names and calculating the right options to buy, I should have thrown all my notes in the trash and just bought soxl. Even if this is the bubble top and soxl is about to go down 90%, I know from experience my stock picks will go down 95%. So I stay may as well buy soxl.

62426144
If I'm just starting out with this stuff, what's wrong with 70% VTI / 30% VXUS?

62426154
Honestly I’m seeing a lot of upside to going into QQQ over VOO as long as you are okay with the fact it is more volatile
The upside it’s just so huge when compounded

62426173
>>62426154
Or honestly, as someone already in VOO, I wonder if it makes sense to just go into something like VGT which has consistently beaten VOO and QQQ (for now anyway) or something like DRAM
A lot of VGTs holdings are currently down but it’s YTD is higher

62426183
>>62407078
I keep half of my funds in VTWAX, rebalancing annually. Right now I'm down 0.84% this year, my portfolio is fucked but VTWAX is my safety net.

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

item.title: Crypto posters keep acting like it's 2018 and crypto is still relevant — 10 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto posters keep acting like it's 2018 and crypto is still relevant

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-30 01:03 UTC

OP

It's pretty sad. (Manufactured) drama about this shitcoin, that shitcoin, this pumper, this dumper--no one, not even them, cares about this anymore.
Everyone knows it's not the
>future of money/contracts/finance
and that it's just some speculative commodity, the digital equivalent of a shiny rock, but without any of the industrial or ornamental applications thos rocks often have.
Actual investors stick to stocks, bonds, and real estate.
Speculators prefer metals, commodities, or some kind of collectable (like Pokemon cards).
Crypto is also OLD. Going on two decades.
And irrelevant.
Good luck trying to "redpill" Gen Alpha on this.

Early replies (first 3)

62425278
>>62425276
ur dumb

62425280
>>62425276
The best part is that you don't even have to pretend to feel sorry for them, since they always knew it was a scam (their memes admit as much), and they just hoped to pawn it off to some Greater Fool before the rug got pulled out from under them.

62425282
>>62425276
First time?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425315
>>62425276
crypto was never relevant

62428452
>>62425315
Ten years ago, it was.
You could hype it as the
>the future of ___
>the next ___
with at least some plausibility.
But now it's almost 20 years old, and besides facilitating cyber crime, it still does nothing.

62428466
it's called volatility chud. it's what makes investing in stocks so profitable. the introduction of perpetual crypto futures is just causing a new wave of weak hands to get shook out. at some point bitcoins limited supply will become a fact again.

62428505
>bitcoin PLUMMETS to just 78k!!!!1!!!!!

62428509
>>62428505
>>bitcoin PLUMMETS to just 78k!!!!1!!!!!
If Microsoft went down to $200 it would be insane. For some reason you think this doesn't apply to crypto.

62428795
>>62428509
So…. the price it was at 4 years ago? You really don’t know shit and it shows, just hilarious you tried to chime in like your input was valuable.

62428828
I feel sorry for shiny rock collectors, their life is juste cope and denial

item.title: Anyone else stacking cardboard? It's the smartest play you can be making right now - the prices are crazy, like BAYC 5 y… — 18 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else stacking cardboard? It's the smartest play you can be making right now - the prices are crazy, like BAYC 5 y…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-30 00:17 UTC

OP

Anyone else stacking cardboard? It's the smartest play you can be making right now - the prices are crazy, like BAYC 5 years ago, except cardboard is actually going to continue to moon forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62428300
People said this about comic books in the 60s, 70s, and 80s, and then it all came crashing down in the 90s. Modern aesthetics don't age well.

62428317
>>62428300
Cards have something that comics, Beanie Babies, and Pez dispensers didn't have: modern social media to peddle unlicensed financial advice.

62428336
>>62428288
classic bubble

as soon as boomers die and younger generations get kicked out of the house you're going to see this stuff crash real quick

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62428347
>>62428336
Cool. That's 20 years from now. Name one other asset with 25 straight years (the past 5 and the next 20) of exponential gains.

62428348
>>62428300
>>62428317
niggers don't realize that Pokemon is the #1 franchise on Earth. Star Wars franchise is a tiny microscopic ant compared to Pokemon. Pokemon is multiple times larger than all Disney properties combined, that includes SW, Marvel, animation, etc, there is no comparison.
Also niggers always forget that trading cards have been around since the 1880's, there is a 150 year track record, almost as long as the stock exchange

62428355
>>62428347
>exponential gains
you don't seem to understand this term

62428357
>>62428348
Tbf, they were worthless for a century (until the 1980s) and weren't beating traditional assets until late 2020.

That being said, it's only up from here.

62428358
>>62428348
>trading cards have been around since the 1880's,
this is true, but the vast majority of 1880's trading cards are still worthless.

62428360
>>62428288
I like some of the old designs and those cards are common & cheap. Not paying more than $2 for a piece of 20 year old cardboard but good luck to those who do.

62428379
>>62428288
I remember when entire binders of this set (fireworks holo ony) were being sold for about $300. I thought that, if I waited, I would find it at a lower price. No way am I going to spend hundreds of thousands for the same thing. There needs to be someone willing to print replicas of this exact holo pattern, but with quality card backing. I personally missed out, I started collecting around Skyridge. Then, the tcg pretty much died out here.

62428388
>>62428358
>>62428357
Trading cards didn't become great until the 1952 Topp's baseball set, up until then 1880's-1951 they didn't even try with the cards
One of the main reasons Pokemon TCG blew up a few years ago was the introduction of IR and SIR's, full art rares. Pokemon finally started making the cards into actual art-work and people loved it, it will only continue to grow.
One Piece TCG also blew up for same reason, their rare's are beautiful to look at irl, the top tier cards shine like it has jewels imbedded, and the artwork is amazing.
It is not a fad or anything, they actually started caring about the artwork and people responded, just like baseball cards blew up in 1952 when they started trying

62428427
>>62428388
what what a post

its a bubble everyone trading cards will collapase within 365 days sell ur shit close ur shops it's time to move on

62428431
>>62428348
Not buying your basedllennial bags, poketard.

62428441
>>62428431
>>62428427
COME SEE MY CARDS

62428462
>>62428388
>up until then 1880's-1951 they didn't even try with the cards
this is perhaps one of the dumbest takes I've read on the topic ever.

But then I actively collect and sell 1800's trading cards so I'm a bit biased.

62428468
>>62428462
you're right, I was exaggerating, I don't know much about old baseball cards
Is it a good time to buy 1800's cards? They seem really cheap, most of them are 50-100 dollars from what I see

62428635
>>62428468
most 1800's cards aren't sports cards

but no, I don't expect them to go up in value much after this long. Not because they're ugly or low effort, but because almost nobody collects them.

62428679
buy vintage cards. psa 9s and ungraded.

item.title: Well since it's becoming apparent all debt payers are suckers, what are the primary means of combating collapsing good g… — 38 replies — 13.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Well since it's becoming apparent all debt payers are suckers, what are the primary means of combating collapsing good g…

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-29 22:42 UTC

OP

Well since it's becoming apparent all debt payers are suckers, what are the primary means of combating collapsing good goy score penalties resulting there-in-of?
Surely there are alternatives to getting things done without the threat of the bad good goy score stopping you.
>No Anon noooooooo you need to have a high good goy score! Get a credit card and start PUMPING it with debt IMMEDIATELY and then pay it off but not too fast so they can get some interest back! Do this for 20 years and then maybe you'll be able to pull out a mortgage!"

Haha nope, it's too late, all too fucked AND gay for that now.

Early replies (first 3)

62423651
>>62423592
Never took a loan, never had a credit card, never owed anyone anything. When the bank sees me depositing another 10k from oil shorts it begs me with ads to put it in some low-% scam account that will barely match inflation. Nope! Shit's out of my account by next Monday, cya suckers!

62423705
What did you expect to happen in an economy where no one can find a fucking job and the cost of living doesn't even remotely resemble what it was like in 2019?

You know what will help someone in this situation? Another five-round interview process where they still get a rejection email at the end.

62423715
>>62423705
Everyone can get a job lol the job reports are great only retards can’t get jobs

I literally just accepted a job offer after two weeks of looking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423859
Do I really have to tap the sign?

62423868
>>62423754
The trick with this kind of shit is to just not have a mailing address. Bail on your cards in between a move or something next time so they can't actually hit you with anything. They sued you because they knew you were a good goy who would ply ball with them. You think they sue jamal and shanequa? Never play ball and ignore literally all mail from them if you can't afford to do some shit like this you cant afford to debt max

62423884
>>62423859
a quarter of Americans aren't paying it back. If they "Win" and collapse the system then I'm the sucker.
A Quarter of Americans is a big number, that's more than enough to win a civil war. The ability to "punish" that amount of people becomes less and less of a reality the more they grow.

62423906
>>62423651
based

62423909
>>62423592
>what are the primary means of combating collapsing good goy score penalties resulting there-in-of?
I have never been rich enough to move out of my mom's house and 80% of my credit card debt is on 0% APR until March 2028 cards. There is no incentive for me to not continue shifting the debt around forever until I become rich.

62423911
>>62423715
even mcdonalds wont give u an interview in 2026 its cooked unc.

62423923
>>62423868
this

just ignore it until they literally break down ur door, even if they come knocking pretend u arent home kek wtf are they gonna do

62423927
>>62423849
>You took out the debt because you were perennially underpaid and had living costs that exceeded pay despite cutting those costs judiciously, and spending carefully and sparingly in line with a carefully-planned budget.
That's right anon I'm surprised you know me so well.

62423950
>>62423868
>>62423923
Nah, they can get a judgment even if they never serve you notice that they're suing you - they just have to lie and say they did, and the default as far as the court is concerned is that it's accepted unless you challenge it, which you won't, because you didn't know you were being sued. AFAIK the only defense then is that you move to vacate the judgment within n days of it being entered because you weren't served, but I don't know if that actually works because the judge basically ignored everything I said to him. NLA.
That's the trick: when you walk into court, the judge is a lawyer and the debt collector is a lawyer and you are not a lawyer. Law? You think law rules? Those two rely on each other for their careers. You're just the mark.

62423989
>>62423592
It doesn't matter if your goyscore is rekt, you will not get a loan for a decent house. You might get enough to fix up a place but you'll be doing all the work. I make $52k a year in a really cheap city. Credit score is 814, no debt. Luckily my rent is around 16% of my salary. Saved up $40k for down payment. Banks won't lend me even $65k to buy a house. I don't earn enough. They'll let me buy an $80k truck at 17% though. Of course I put the savings into the market for now but I'm only really breaking even at this point.

62424019
>>62423884
I haven't paid student loans for 15 years I just apply for all the deferred options everytime because I saw it getting bad enough that eventually they'll wave it, or the 4-5% I'm accruing interest is lower than what I make using that money instead.
>>62423859
Tell that to fucks like JP Morgan or Citibank that took free loans from the government after fucking up.
Or fuck that, trump hasn't paid off a loan in decades and he's president. Why the fuck would I be a sucker with that as a role model?

62424047
>>62424019
You do know the banks paid back the loans with interest, right?

62424057
>>62423592
You should have got a (((PPP))) loan. They were all forgiven :^)

62424092
>goy score

It's quite something that your people can kill a third of mine and we can forgive you, but when we put a leash on your retarded ass to stop you from hurting yourself you lose your shit.

62424159
>>62423909
This
There's a million credit card offers with 1-2 years of zero interest intro rate and zero balance transfer fee

62424261
>>62424092
>We can forgive you
>Prevent you from hurting yourselves

62424417
>>62424047
>"Interest"
Sweetheart deal. And they never paid reparations for the millions of financial lives they ruined. And they used their continued existence to scam even more people through fraudulent foreclosures. And none of their executives went to jail.

62426001
i'm paying off my $150k loan in the spring. it's all a jewnigger scam
>plan future around SAVE payments of like $600/mo
>orange nigger and the kike congress unilaterally change the terms of payment
>SAVE killed and payments go up to like $1k/mo
oh and of course you can't pay via credit card to get a modest 1% cash back.

62426192
>>62424261
He means stop you from putting too many people into debt. Jews/bankers often get killed when it happens. Because law enforcement can't enforce anything when the number of delinquents reaches critical mass and are also constantly at risk of becoming one of them. It just gets to the point where it is easier to go after the creditors. And that is the best case scinaro before it get to the point of lynch mobs.

62426210
>>62423859
Did society give that graduate a high paying job to pay it back? Are there too many graduates in his situation? Take the L or prepare to die.

62426367
>>62426210
Yes it's our fault for offering people debt they accepted

62428028
Friendly reminder that the ultimate beneficiaries of tuition gouging isn't the banks, the government, or "da jooz", but the universities, most of which have eye-watering endowments already, and are the ones selling the actual useless degrees made up of 60% left-wing propaganda instead of actual job training.

If you want to direct your anger at or suggest violence towards someone, it ought to be them.

62428045
>>62426367
>>62428028
Shut the fuck up, kikes. Now face the wall.

62428078
>>62423592
Student loans are shackles on young people. It's no wonder people aren't starting families. My student loan payments are astronomical. I could definitely afford a house if I didn't have to pay student loans

62428157
>>62428078
If you didn't have enough sense to get a high paying career and/or your parents aren't rich, you shouldn't expect to ever have your own house.

62428164
>>62426210
dangerously based

62428186
>>62428045
See>>62428157

62428239
>>62428223
Are you…poor..?

62428356
>>62423592
>>62423651

You're leaving money on the table.

There's no interest charged to your credit card if you pay off all payments on the statement balance.

You're leaving money on the table because if you get a card with good cash back benefits, it's money back to you, on money that otherwise would not get that by just paying things with your debit card.

I have a couple cards that give 3-6 % cash back depending on the purchase. Like gas or groceries.

62428423
>>62428356
Rich get richer.
